license.title: Software License
license.markdownDescription:
- Specifies the license or type field of the software
- If different files have different licenses, separate them with a comma `,`
- If the entire application is multi-licensed, separate them with `|`
- https://spdx.org/licenses
- https://opensource.org/license

identifier.title: License Identifier
identifier.markdownDescription:
- Specifies license of software
- If different file have different license, separate with comma `,`
- If entire application is multi-licensed, separate with `|`
- https://spdx.org/licenses
- https://opensource.org/license

### Special Value

- `Unknown`: [Special value](https://github.com/ScoopInstaller/Scoop/wiki/App-Manifests#required-properties) defined by [Scoop](https://scoop.sh)
  - Unable to determine license: `unable to determine the license`
- `Freeware`: [Special value](https://github.com/ScoopInstaller/Scoop/wiki/App-Manifests#required-properties) defined by [Scoop](https://scoop.sh)
  - Freeware: `free to use forever`
- `Public Domain`: [Special value](https://github.com/ScoopInstaller/Scoop/wiki/App-Manifests#required-properties) defined by [Scoop](https://scoop.sh)
  - Public domain software: `Public Domain`
- `Shareware`: [Special value](https://github.com/ScoopInstaller/Scoop/wiki/App-Manifests#required-properties) defined by [Scoop](https://scoop.sh)
  - Trialware, must pay after trial period end: `free to try, must pay eventually`
- `Proprietary`: [Special value](https://github.com/ScoopInstaller/Scoop/wiki/App-Manifests#required-properties) defined by [Scoop](https://scoop.sh)
  - Commercial software: `must pay to use`
  - [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com) extend definition:
    - Proprietary commercial software, whether payment is required or not
    - Software defined as `Proprietary` in [winget-pkgs](https://github.com/microsoft/winget-pkgs)
- `Freemium`: Special value defined by [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com)
  - Basic feature free, pay to unlock advanced feature: `free to use, must pay to unlock advanced features`

notes_cn.title: Notes (Chinese)
notes_cn.markdownDescription:
- Specifies the **Chinese** notes/messages to display after installation
- It will be displayed every time the software is successfully installed

### [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com)

- When `version` is set to the following special values defined by [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com), it will also serve as a relevant descriptive note:
  - `pending`
  - `renamed`
  - `deprecated`

- Variables can be used, which will be automatically replaced when displayed:

    |Available Variables|Description|
    |:-:|-|
    |`$app`|Application name (manifest name)|
    |`$architecture`|CPU architecture, e.g., `64bit`, `32bit`, `arm64`|
    |`$cmd`|Currently running subcommand, e.g., `uninstall`, `update`, `install`|
    |`$fname`|Downloaded filename (could be multiple files, needs to be handled as an array unless you are sure the URL is not an array)|
    |`$scoopConfig`|Scoop configuration (PowerShell object), e.g., {SCOOP_BRANCH, SCOOP_REPO}|
    |`$global`|`$true` if installed with `-g` or `--global`|
    |`$manifest`|Deserialized manifest (PowerShell object), e.g., @{version=1.2.3; description=xxx;...}|
    |`$version`|The version being installed, e.g., 1.2.3|
    |`$dir`|Application installation directory (`current` directory)|
    |`$persist_dir`|Data persistence directory|
    |`$bucketsdir`|Scoop buckets directory, e.g., D:\scoop\buckets|
    |`$bucket`|The name of the bucket the currently installed application belongs to|
    |`$cachedir`|Scoop cache directory|
    |`$configFile`|Path to the Scoop configuration file|
    |`$globaldir`|Global installation directory|
    |`$original_dir`|Application installation directory (actual version-numbered directory)|
    |`$scoopdir`|The directory where Scoop is located|

depends.title: Dependencies
depends.markdownDescription:
- Specifies the required dependencies for the software
- Uninstalled dependencies will be automatically installed before the software installation
- If dependencies are optional, use the `suggest` property

allOf.errorMessage: Can only depend on manifests in abyss, e.g., abyss/xxx.yyy

items.errorMessage: Can only depend on manifests in abyss, e.g., abyss/xxx.yyy

suggest.title: Suggested Dependencies
suggest.markdownDescription:
- Displays a note suggesting users install optional software
- Each object represents a **feature suggestion**, and its value is the software that implements it:
  - String: Specifies a single software
  - Array: Specifies multiple software, installing any one of them is sufficient
- If any software corresponding to the **feature suggestion** is already installed, the feature is considered **fulfilled**, and the user will not see the suggestion

url.title: Download URL
url.markdownDescription:
- Specifies the URL of the file to download
- If there are multiple URLs, an array can be used (archives should come first)

  ```json
  "url": [
      "https://example.com/file1.zip",
      "https://example.com/file2.exe"
  ]
  ```

- If you need to change the filename of the downloaded URL, you can append `#/program.exe` to the end of the URL

  ```json
  "url": "https://example.org/program.1.2.3.exe#/program.exe"
  ```

url_autoupdate.title: Download URL
url_autoupdate.markdownDescription:
- Specifies the URL of the file to download
- If there are multiple URLs, an array can be used (archives should come first)

  ```json
  "url": [
      "https://example.com/file1.zip",
      "https://example.com/file2.exe"
  ]
  ```

- If you need to change the filename of the downloaded URL, you can append `#/program.exe` to the end of the URL

  ```json
  "url": "https://example.org/program.1.2.3.exe#/program.exe"
  ```

hash.title: Hash Value
hash.markdownDescription:
- Specifies the hash value of the software, defaulting to `sha256`
- Other hash algorithms are also supported by adding the corresponding prefix to the hash string:
  - `sha512:<hash>`
  - `sha1:<hash>`
  - `md5:<hash>`

hash_autoupdate.markdownDescription: Defines how to get the hash

mode.markdownDescription:
Hash Mode

- `github`: Get the automatically generated hash from GitHub; for details, see [GitHub Changelog](https://github.blog/changelog/2025-06-03-releases-now-expose-digests-for-release-assets/)
- `json`: Extract the hash from a JSON file using a JSONPath expression
- `xpath`: Extract the hash from an XML file

url.markdownDescription: URL template used to download RDF/JSON files or extract hashes

regex.markdownDescription: RegEx expression to extract the hash

jsonpath.markdownDescription:
Get the version using a JSON endpoint with a JSONPath expression, using either dot notation or bracket notation.

### Example

- Extract the value of the `version` property

  ```json
  "jsonpath": "$.version"
  ```

- Extract the value of the `productVersion` or `version` property

  ```json
  "jsonpath": "$...[?(@.productVersion || @.version)]"
  ```

- Extract `updated_at` from the property in `assets` where `name` is `xxx`

  ```json
  "jsonpath": "$.assets[?(@.name == 'xxx')].updated_at"
  ```

- Extract the values of `productVersion` and `version` properties into an array, e.g., `"["1.2.3","1.2.3-rc"]"`

  ```json
  "jsonpath": "$..['productVersion','version']"
  ```

xpath.markdownDescription: XPath expression to extract the hash value

architecture.title: Architecture Info
architecture.markdownDescription:
- Specifies installation configurations under different architectures. Supported architectures include:
  - `64bit`: 64-bit
  - `32bit`: 32-bit
  - `arm64`: ARM 64-bit
- When `architecture` is specified, properties within it will override top-level properties of the same name

64bit.title: 64-bit
64bit.markdownDescription:
- Specifies the installation configuration for 64-bit architecture
- Common architecture identifiers in URLs: `x64`, `x86_64`, `amd64`, `win64`

32bit.title: 32-bit
32bit.markdownDescription:
- Specifies the installation configuration for 32-bit architecture
- Common architecture identifiers in URLs: `x86`, `i386`, `i686`, `win32`

allOf.errorMessage: Not allowed in abyss

arm64.title: ARM 64-bit
arm64.markdownDescription:
- Specifies the installation configuration for ARM 64-bit architecture
- Common architecture identifiers in URLs: `arm64`, `aarch64`

extract_dir.title: Extract Specified Directory
extract_dir.markdownDescription:
- Specifies which contents inside the archive to extract
- By default, Scoop extracts all contents from the archive
  - Archive types: `.zip`/`.7z`/`.tar`/`.gz`/`.lzma`/`.lzh`
- If the `extract_dir` property is present, Scoop will only extract the specified directory defined by it

extract_to.title: Extract to Specified Directory
extract_to.markdownDescription:
- If the value of `url` is an archive, Scoop will extract all contents into the target directory defined by `extract_to`
  - Archive types: `.zip`/`.7z`/`.tar`/`.gz`/`.lzma`/`.lzh`
- If `url` is an array containing multiple archives, `extract_to` should also be an array matching the order of the `url` array

### [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com)

- If the value of `url` is a single file, `extract_to` will move it into the specified directory
  - Single file types: `.ps1`/`.bat`/`.cmd`
  - When no `A-Install-*` method is used, `.exe` is also treated as a single file
- If `url` is an array containing both archives and single files, archives must come first. Reference [JanDeDobbeleer.OhMyPosh](https://github.com/abgox/abyss/blob/main/bucket/j/JanDeDobbeleer/JanDeDobbeleer.OhMyPosh.json)

env_set.title: Set Environment Variables
env_set.markdownDescription:
- Sets one or more **User-level** environment variables
- If the `--global`/`-g` parameter is used during installation, they will be set as **System-level** environment variables
- Some available variables:
  - `$app`: Manifest name
  - `$dir`: Software installation directory
  - `$persist_dir`: Data persistence directory
  - `$home`: User home directory
  - `$env:AppData`: Windows environment variable
  - ...

allOf.errorMessage: Empty string is not allowed for property names

env_set_shared.title: Set Environment Variables (Shared)
env_set_shared.markdownDescription:
- It is a special property of [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com)
- Sets one or more **User-level** environment variables
- If the `--global`/`-g` parameter is used during installation, they will be set as **System-level** environment variables
### Difference from env_set
- It requires specifying the owners of these environment variables
- When the app is uninstalled, the environment variable will not be removed if owners still exist

allOf.errorMessage: Empty string is not allowed for property names

value.title: Environment Variable Value
value.markdownDescription:
- Equivalent to the value set by `env_set`
- Some available variables:
  - `$app`: Manifest name
  - `$dir`: Software installation directory
  - `$persist_dir`: Data persistence directory
  - `$home`: User home directory
  - `$env:AppData`: Windows environment variable
  - ...

owner.title: Environment Variable Owner
owner.markdownDescription:
- It is the major difference from `env_set`; it requires specifying the owners of these environment variables
- When the app is uninstalled, the environment variable will not be removed if owners still exist

env_add_path.title: Environment Variable PATH
env_add_path.markdownDescription:
- Specifies paths to be added to the [PATH environment variable](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/path)
- Paths are relative to the installation directory `$dir`. To specify the installation directory itself, use `.`
- Added to **User-level** by default; if the `--global`/`-g` parameter is used during installation, it will be added to **System-level**
- If you need to include variables like `$home`, `$env:AppData`, please use `env_add_path_expand`

then.errorMessage:
It cannot add paths containing variables
Please use env_add_path_expand

allOf.errorMessage:
It cannot add paths containing variables
Please use env_add_path_expand

env_add_path_expand.title: Environment Variable PATH (With Variables)
env_add_path_expand.markdownDescription:
- It is a special property of [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com)
- Specifies paths to be added to the [PATH environment variable](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/path)
- Added to **User-level** by default; if the `--global`/`-g` parameter is used during installation, it will be added to **System-level**

### Difference from env_add_path

- It can use variables, including environment variables defined by `env_set`
  - `$app`: Manifest name
  - `$dir`: Software installation directory
  - `$persist_dir`: Data persistence directory
  - `$home`: User home directory
  - `$env:AppData`: Windows environment variable
  - ...
- It follows an ownership mechanism: added variables will only be deleted by the adder

allOf.errorMessage: Please use env_add_path to add paths without variables

items.errorMessage: Please use env_add_path to add paths without variables

psmodule.title: PowerShell Module
psmodule.markdownDescription:
- Specifies the PowerShell module to be installed in `$scoopdir/modules`
- The module name must match the following filenames exactly (excluding extension):
  - Module script file: `<name>.psm1`
  - Module manifest file: `<name>.psd1`
- Reference [PowerShell Module Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/powershell/scripting/developer/module/how-to-write-a-powershell-module-manifest)

### Example

- Taking [PSCompletions](https://pscompletions.abgox.com) as an example
  - Module script file: `PSCompletions.psm1`
  - Module manifest file: `PSCompletions.psd1`

- Therefore, its module name is `PSCompletions`

name.title: Module Name
name.markdownDescription:
- Specifies the name of the PowerShell module
- Must match the following filenames exactly (excluding extension):
  - Module script file: `<name>.psm1`
  - Module manifest file: `<name>.psd1`
- Reference [PowerShell Module Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/powershell/scripting/developer/module/how-to-write-a-powershell-module-manifest)

### Example

- Taking [PSCompletions](https://pscompletions.abgox.com) as an example
  - Module script file: `PSCompletions.psm1`
  - Module manifest file: `PSCompletions.psd1`

- Therefore, its module name is `PSCompletions`

font.title: Font
font.markdownDescription:
- It is a special property of [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com)
- It will be installed as a font manifest
- Typically set to `true` unless there is an explicit need to specify the font type
  - `true`: Extract appropriate font files from the font package
  - `ttf`: Extract `ttf` font files from the font package
  - `otf`: Extract `otf` font files from the font package
  - `ttc`: Extract `ttc` font files from the font package

msix.title: appx/msix/msixbundle
msix.markdownDescription:
- It is a special property of [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com)
- It is used to install the following types of packages:
  - `msixbundle`
  - `msix`
  - `appx`
- It requires `PackageFamilyName` as its value

### Execution

- Executes installation logic via `A-Start-Install` in `pre_install`
- Executes uninstallation logic via `A-Start-Uninstall` in `pre_uninstall`
- You can also manually invoke `A-Install-MsixPackage` and `A-Uninstall-MsixPackage`

### Example

- Install PowerShell

  ```json
  "msix": "Microsoft.PowerShell_8wekyb3d8bbwe"
  ```

bin.markdownDescription:
Program (executable file or script) string or array of strings

- A string or string array representing programs (executables or scripts) that should be made available in the user's PATH
- If it is a Graphical User Interface (GUI) application and does not accept any command-line arguments, there is no need to add it to `bin`

### Example

1. Scoop will create a shim named `a`, allowing the `a` command to be run directly to execute `a.exe`

   ```json
   "bin": "a.exe"
   ```

2. Similar to example 1, but Scoop will create two shims: `a` and `b`

   ```json
   "bin": ["a.exe", "b.exe"]
   ```

3. Similar to example 2, but the names of the two created shims will be changed to `ax` and `bx`

```json
    "bin": [
        [
            "a.exe",
            "ax"
        ],
        [
            "b.exe",
            "bx"
        ]
    ]
```

4. Similar to example 3, but when running them, they will carry the `--config $dir/config.json` arguments

```json
"bin": [
    [
        "a.exe",
        "ax",
        "--config $dir/config.json"
    ],
    [
        "b.exe",
        "bx",
        "--config $dir/config.json"
    ]
]
```

shortcuts.title: Shortcuts
shortcuts.markdownDescription:
- Specifies shortcuts to be added to the Start Menu, organized under the `Scoop Apps` directory
- Each shortcut is an array containing the following values in order:
  1. **Target File Path** (Required): Path relative to the installation directory `$dir`
  2. **Shortcut Name** (Required): Supports subdirectories, e.g., `Subdirectory\Name`
  3. **Application Launch Arguments** (Optional)
  4. **Icon File Path** (Optional): Path relative to the installation directory `$dir`

link.markdownDescription:
Data Persistence [link](https://abyss.abgox.com/docs/features/data-persistence/link)

- It is a special property of [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com)
- Used to persist data stored in standard data directories (such as `$env:AppData`, `$home`, etc.)
- It maps paths to the corresponding directory structure in `$persist_dir` according to [Link Rules](https://abyss.abgox.com/docs/features/data-persistence/link-rule)
- Executed automatically in `A-Start-Install`, can also be manually invoked using `A-New-Link`
- Since Scoop defaults to creating empty directories only, to persist files you need to prepare default data in the `extra` directory in advance

### Example

- Taking [VSCode](https://code.visualstudio.com) as an example, its manifest is `Microsoft.VisualStudioCode`

  ```json
    "link": [
        "$env:AppData\\Code",
        "$home\\.vscode"
    ]
  ```

- Link Targets:
  - `$env:AppData\Code` => `$persist_dir\AppData\Roaming\Code`
  - `$home\.vscode` => `$persist_dir\.vscode`

- Before linking, [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com) prepares data in `$persist_dir` in the following order:
  1. If data exists in the original path, migrate it directly to `$persist_dir`
  2. If default data exists in the `extra\Microsoft.VisualStudioCode` directory, copy it to `$persist_dir`
     - `$bucketsdir\$bucket\extra\Microsoft.VisualStudioCode\AppData\Roaming\Code` => `$persist_dir\AppData\Roaming\Code`
     - `$bucketsdir\$bucket\extra\Microsoft.VisualStudioCode\.vscode` => `$persist_dir\.vscode`
  3. If no data is found, an empty directory is created directly (default Scoop behavior)

persist.markdownDescription:
Data Persistence [persist](https://abyss.abgox.com/docs/features/data-persistence/persist)

- Specifies directories or files that need to be persisted, preventing data loss after software updates or reinstallation
- Scoop points the paths inside `$dir` to the corresponding paths in `$persist_dir` by creating links
- Since Scoop defaults to creating empty directories only, to persist files you need to prepare default data in the `extra` directory in advance

### Example

- Taking [PSCompletions](https://pscompletions.abgox.com) as an example, its manifest is `abgox.PSCompletions`
- You need to prepare the file `extra\abgox.PSCompletions\data.json` to prevent Scoop from creating a directory by default

  ```json
  "persist": [
      ["completions","completions"],
      ["data.json","data.json"]
  ]
  ```

- Link Targets:
  - `$dir\completions` => `$persist_dir\completions`
  - `$dir\data.json` => `$persist_dir\data.json`
- Before linking, [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com) prepares data in `$persist_dir` in the following order:
  1. If data exists in `$dir`, migrate it directly to `$persist_dir`
  2. If default data exists in the `extra\abgox.PSCompletions` directory, copy it to `$persist_dir`
     - `$bucketsdir\$bucket\extra\abgox.PSCompletions\completions` => `$persist_dir\completions`
     - `$bucketsdir\$bucket\extra\abgox.PSCompletions\data.json` => `$persist_dir\data.json`
  3. If no data is found, an empty directory is created directly (default Scoop behavior)

### Limitations

- If default data is stored under standard data directories, you must switch to [link](https://abyss.abgox.com/docs/features/data-persistence#link) instead
  - `$env:AppData\xxx`
  - `$env:LocalAppData\xxx`
  - `$home\xxx`
  - ...
- Even if the software provides environment variables to customize data storage locations, [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com) does not allow it to impact the stability of the directory structure under `$persist_dir`
- For portable software, you can continue using `persist`, but its definition is restricted
  - Assuming the default data directory is `$env:AppData\xxx`
  - According to [Link Rules](https://abyss.abgox.com/docs/features/data-persistence/link-rule), the target directory is `$persist_dir\AppData\Roaming\xxx`
  - Therefore, it can only use the following definition, where `AppData\Roaming\xxx` remains immutable

  ```json
  "persist": [
    ["<source>","AppData\\Roaming\\xxx"]
  ]
  ```

cleanup.title: Clean Up Temporary Data
cleanup.markdownDescription:
- It is a special property of [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com)
- Specifies temporary data that needs to be cleaned up
- Only cleaned up when uninstalling the app; ignored during updates
- If there are multiple items, use an array

### Example

- [Tauri](https://tauri.app) typically uses `$env:LocalAppData\com.xxx.app` to store `EBWebView` and `logs`

  ```json
  "cleanup": "$env:LocalAppData\\com.xxx.app"
  ```

- [Electron](https://electronjs.org) typically uses `$env:LocalAppData\xxx-updater` to store the updater

  ```json
  "cleanup": "$env:LocalAppData\\xxx-updater"
  ```

admin.title: Requires Administrator Privileges
admin.markdownDescription:
- It is a special property of [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com)
- When set to `true`, terminates the current operation if the user does not have administrator privileges
- Reference: https://abyss.abgox.com/docs/require-admin

pre_install.title: Pre-installation Script
pre_install.markdownDescription:
- Executes commands before the software installation
- Order of execution: `pre_install` -> `installer` -> `post_install`

|Available Variables|Description|
|:-:|-|
|`$app`|Application name (manifest name)|
|`$architecture`|CPU architecture, e.g., `64bit`, `32bit`, `arm64`|
|`$cmd`|Currently running subcommand, e.g., `uninstall`, `update`, `install`|
|`$fname`|Downloaded filename (could be multiple files, needs to be handled as an array unless you are sure the URL is not an array)|
|`$scoopConfig`|Scoop configuration (PowerShell object), e.g., {SCOOP_BRANCH, SCOOP_REPO}|
|`$global`|`$true` if installed with `-g` or `--global`|
|`$manifest`|Deserialized manifest (PowerShell object), e.g., @{version=1.2.3; description=xxx;...}|
|`$version`|The version being installed, e.g., 1.2.3|
|`$dir`|Application installation directory (actual version-numbered directory)|
|`$persist_dir`|Data persistence directory|
|`$bucketsdir`|Scoop buckets directory, e.g., D:\scoop\buckets|
|`$bucket`|The name of the bucket the currently installed application belongs to|
|`$cachedir`|Scoop cache directory|
|`$configFile`|Path to the Scoop configuration file|
|`$globaldir`|Global installation directory|
|`$original_dir`|Application installation directory (actual version-numbered directory)|
|`$scoopdir`|The directory where Scoop is located|

Use installer

- `-Uninstaller`: Path to the uninstaller
  - If it is a relative path, it will automatically concatenate into an absolute path
  - If the `location` property is present, it concatenates `location` as the parent directory
  - Otherwise, it concatenates `$dir` as the parent directory
- `-ArgumentList`: Command-line arguments for the installer
  - Defaults to `'/S', '/CurrentUser', "/D=$dir\app"`
  - If the `admin` property is `true`, `'/CurrentUser'` is removed from defaults
  - If the `location` property is present, `"/D=$dir\app"` is removed from defaults
- `-SleepSec`: Seconds to wait after the installer finishes running
  - Defaults to `3`

### Example

- For applications developed with [Tauri](https://tauri.app)

  ```json
  "A-Install-App -Uninstaller 'app\\uninstall.exe'"
  ```

post_install.title: Post-installation Script
post_install.markdownDescription:
- Executes commands after software installation
- Order of execution: `pre_install` -> `installer` -> `post_install`
- In [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com), it must be a specified fixed array
- If custom installation logic must be added in `post_install`, ensure the last item is `A-Complete-Install`

|Available Variables|Description|
|:-:|-|
|`$app`|Application name (manifest name)|
|`$architecture`|CPU architecture, e.g., `64bit`, `32bit`, `arm64`|
|`$cmd`|Currently running subcommand, e.g., `uninstall`, `update`, `install`|
|`$fname`|Downloaded filename (could be multiple files, needs to be handled as an array unless you are sure the URL is not an array)|
|`$scoopConfig`|Scoop configuration (PowerShell object), e.g., {SCOOP_BRANCH, SCOOP_REPO}|
|`$global`|`$true` if installed with `-g` or `--global`|
|`$manifest`|Deserialized manifest (PowerShell object), e.g., @{version=1.2.3; description=xxx;...}|
|`$version`|The version being installed, e.g., 1.2.3|
|`$dir`|Application installation directory (`current`)|
|`$persist_dir`|Data persistence directory|
|`$bucketsdir`|Scoop buckets directory, e.g., D:\scoop\buckets|
|`$bucket`|The name of the bucket the currently installed application belongs to|
|`$cachedir`|Scoop cache directory|
|`$configFile`|Path to the Scoop configuration file|
|`$globaldir`|Global installation directory|
|`$original_dir`|Application installation directory (actual version-numbered directory)|
|`$scoopdir`|The directory where Scoop is located|

post_uninstall.title: Post-uninstallation Script
post_uninstall.markdownDescription:
- Executes commands after software uninstallation
- Order of execution: `pre_uninstall` -> `uninstaller` -> `post_uninstall`
- In [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com), it must be a specified fixed array
- If custom uninstallation logic must be added in `post_uninstall`, ensure the last item is `A-Complete-Uninstall`

|Available Variables|Description|
|:-:|-|
|`$app`|Application name (manifest name)|
|`$architecture`|CPU architecture, e.g., `64bit`, `32bit`, `arm64`|
|`$cmd`|Currently running subcommand, e.g., `uninstall`, `update`, `install`|
|`$fname`|Downloaded filename (could be multiple files, needs to be handled as an array unless you are sure the URL is not an array)|
|`$scoopConfig`|Scoop configuration (PowerShell object), e.g., {SCOOP_BRANCH, SCOOP_REPO}|
|`$global`|`$true` if installed with `-g` or `--global`|
|`$manifest`|Deserialized manifest (PowerShell object), e.g., @{version=1.2.3; description=xxx;...}|
|`$version`|The version being installed, e.g., 1.2.3|
|`$dir`|Application installation directory (actual version-numbered directory)|
|`$persist_dir`|Data persistence directory|
|`$bucketsdir`|Scoop buckets directory, e.g., D:\scoop\buckets|
|`$bucket`|The name of the bucket the currently installed application belongs to|
|`$cachedir`|Scoop cache directory|
|`$configFile`|Path to the Scoop configuration file|
|`$globaldir`|Global installation directory|
|`$original_dir`|Application installation directory (actual version-numbered directory)|
|`$scoopdir`|The directory where Scoop is located|

anyOf.markdownDescription:
Get installation info for the specified app in Winget

- Requires two arguments:
  - `PackageIdentifier`: The `PackageIdentifier` of the app in [winget-pkgs](https://github.com/microsoft/winget-pkgs)
  - `InstallerType`: Specifies the installer type suffix to extract the matching installer package from [winget-pkgs](https://github.com/microsoft/winget-pkgs); empty string indicates any type
- `$res`: Installer information parsed from [winget-pkgs](https://github.com/microsoft/winget-pkgs)
- `$out`: Default installation info, providing default version matching content for the `regex` property; can be used with `$res` for custom overrides

### Example

- Retrieve installation info for Microsoft Edge, extracting info for packages with `.msi` URL suffix:

  ```json
  "$res, $out = A-Get-InstallerInfoFromWinget 'Microsoft.Edge' 'msi'"
  ```

regex.markdownDescription:
RegEx (Regular Expression) to find the version

### Example

- By default, stores the contents of the first capture group into `$version`

  ```json
  "regex": "(.+)"
  ```

- Restricts the version format to digits and dots

  ```json
  "regex": "([\\d.]+)"
  ```

- Can use named capture groups to store the version number into `$version`

  ```json
  "regex": "(?<version>[\\d.]+)"
  ```

- Strictly matches the start and end

  ```json
  "regex": "^(?<version>[\\d.]+)$"
  ```

jsonpath.markdownDescription:
Get the version using a JSON endpoint with a JSONPath expression, using either dot notation or bracket notation.

### Example

- Extract the value of the `version` property

  ```json
  "jsonpath": "$.version"
  ```

xpath.markdownDescription: XPath expression to find the version

reverse.markdownDescription: If set to true, matches the last occurrence (defaults to matching the first occurrence)

replace.markdownDescription:
Replace the matched value with a calculated value

### Example

- `${1}.${2}.${3}.${4}${5}${6}`
  - `${1}` indicate first match group
  - `${2}` indicate second match group
  - ...

version.title: Version
version.markdownDescription:
- Specifies the version of the software
- Typically uses [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org), such as `1.2.3`
- `nightly` represents nightly builds, which exhibit the following special behaviors:
  - `scoop update` will **not** automatically detect new versions; an explicit force update via `--force`/`-f` is required
  - Skips file hash verification

### [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com)

- It allows several special values:
  - `pending`: Pending
  - `renamed`: Renamed
  - `deprecated`: Deprecated
  - `virtual`: Virtual Package
- Reference [Manifest Status Control](https://abyss.abgox.com/docs/features/manifest-status-control)

renamed.title: Renamed Manifest
renamed.markdownDescription:
- It is a special property of [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com)
- It defines the old and new names of a renamed manifest
- If there is [link](https://abyss.abgox.com/docs/features/data-persistence/link) or [persist](https://abyss.abgox.com/docs/features/data-persistence/persist) data that needs to be migrated, it must be defined in both the old and new manifests simultaneously

### Example

- Single old manifest name:

  ```json
  "renamed": {
      "new": "nerd-fonts.Hack",
      "old": "nerd-fonts.Hack.NF"
  }
  ```

conflicts.title: Conflicting Manifests
conflicts.markdownDescription:
- It is a special property of [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com)
- It requires an array containing the names of manifests that are mutually exclusive
- Refuses installation if manifests conflict
- Reference: https://abyss.abgox.com/docs/deny-if-app-conflict

### Example

```json
"conflicts": [
    "Microsoft.PowerShell",
    "Microsoft.PowerShell-LTS"
]
```

location.title: External Installation Location
location.markdownDescription:
- It is a special property of [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com)
- Used to specify the target location if the app installer deploys to standard software directories instead of the scoop directory
  - `$env:ProgramFiles\xxx`
  - `${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\xxx`
  - `$env:ProgramData\xxx`
  - `$env:AppData\xxx`
  - `$env:LocalAppData\xxx`
- If the version number is `virtual`, it represents the assumed installation location of the app
- Reference: https://abyss.abgox.com/docs/external-installation-directory

data_shared.title: Data Sharing Manifests
data_shared.markdownDescription:
- It is a special property of [abyss](https://abyss.abgox.com)
- All manifests that share data must define it
- It requires an array containing the names of manifests that share data
- Reference: https://abyss.abgox.com/docs/shared-data

### Example

```json
"data_shared": [
    "Microsoft.PowerShell",
    "Microsoft.PowerShell.Preview"
]
```

autoupdate.title: Auto Update
autoupdate.markdownDescription:
- Automatically updates manifests via [GitHub Actions](https://github.com/ScoopInstaller/GithubActions)
- Scoop allows using `scoop install AppName@1.2.3` to install specific versions
- However, this is only possible if all `url` items in the manifest's `autoupdate` property contain only the following [version variables](https://github.com/ScoopInstaller/Scoop/wiki/App-Manifest-Autoupdate#version-variables):
  - `$version`: `3.7.1.2-rc.1`
    - `$majorVersion`: `3`
    - `$minorVersion`: `7`
    - `$patchVersion`: `1`
    - `$buildVersion`: `2`
    - `$preReleaseVersion`: `rc.1`
  - `$dotVersion`: `3.7.1.2.rc.1`
  - `$dashVersion`: `3-7-1-2-rc-1`
  - `$underscoreVersion`: `3_7_1_2_rc_1`
  - `$cleanVersion`: `3712rc1`
  - `$matchHead`: `3.7.1`
  - `$matchTail`: `.2-rc.1`
